prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins on a very dark, nearly blank screen. A “Classic Commercial Archives” watermark appears in the lower-left corner, and a faint archive watermark is visible across the lower middle.

At around 9.8 seconds, the scene changes to the interior of a car. Two adult men are seated side by side in the front seats. The man on the right, with dark curly hair, looks downward toward an object or package held near his chest; the man on the left also looks down. The image has a strong greenish overlay or reflection across the lower half, making some details hard to distinguish.

At around 17.7 seconds, the setting changes to an indoor home-like room. A woman sits on the left, wearing a light-colored blouse or jacket with a small pattern. A man stands partly visible on the right, shown mainly from the shoulders down. They appear to be near a table or bed with fabric or clothing in the foreground. A lit table lamp and window shutters are visible in the background, suggesting a domestic interior.

At around 25.6 seconds, the commercial cuts to a product display. Several Dial “Very Dry” deodorant/antiperspirant products are arranged upright against a dark background: a large cylindrical container on the left, a box or package in the center, and a roll-on-style container on the right. A pink cap-like object is placed in front. The “dial” logo appears in a large rectangular outline in the upper right. The products feature prominent pink circular graphics.

The final frame remains on the same product lineup. The Dial logo is clear in the upper right, and the packaging text “dial” and “VERY DRY” is visible on the products. The arrangement is static, with the products centered and highlighted against the dark background.
